description: Native app surfaces phase A2 (docs/design/native-app-surfaces.md): startSurface launches macOS desktop apps through the lazily-installed Mac2 driver, find/click/type act on them via the AX semantic mapping, and closeSurface terminates them. TextEdit exercises the bundle-ID launch form plus args (a file path argument that must visibly open, proving the arguments capability); Calculator exercises the .app-path launch form plus AX-column clicks; the env permutation exercises the appium:environment capability path (stock apps offer nothing that renders an env var, so readiness is the assertion — noted here so the weaker check is a documented choice, not an oversight). Gated to macOS hosts; on Windows/Linux every test lands SKIPPED via runOn. The macOS runner needs Accessibility (TCC) granted — without it the app preflight lands the context as SKIPPED with the System Settings walkthrough (asserted behavior on unprovisioned machines, actively granted in CI so these tests PASS).

tests.description: Launch TextEdit by bundle ID with launch arguments that must visibly take effect: the argv user-defaults pair -NSShowAppCentricOpenPanelInsteadOfUntitledFile NO makes TextEdit open an untitled document instead of its open panel, so the text view the readiness check waits on only exists when the args flowed. (A file-path argument was the first attempt; TextEdit is sandboxed and cannot open an arbitrary /tmp path from argv.) Then type into the text view via the native-selector escape hatch, assert the typed content back out via elementText (which matches AXValue on text views), screenshot, and close the surface. autoRecord proves app-context recording on macOS: the capture starts full-display and the first app surface late-binds its window rect as the crop.

Launch Calculator by .app path, click digits through the AX semantic column (elementAria accessible-name matching — the string form; the { role, name } object form is not yet schema-reachable, see ADR 01023) and the operator keys through the native-selector escape hatch (their accessible names are words like Add/Equals, not symbols — the OR-candidates absorb casing differences across macOS versions), verify the result renders via elementText (matches the display's AXValue), screenshot, and close the surface.

Launch Calculator by bundle ID with env set, proving the appium:environment capability path end-to-end. Stock apps render nothing env-derived, so launch readiness is the assertion (documented trade-off in the spec description).
